steps.title: Analyze the first function
steps.content: For $f(x)=-5(4)^x-4$, the exponential part grows as $x$ increases. Multiplying by a negative coefficient makes the outputs decrease without bound, so there is no lowest output value.

steps.title: Analyze the second function
steps.content: For $g(x)=-4(5)^x$, the same one-sided behavior occurs: as $x$ gets larger, the values keep becoming more negative. That rules out a minimum value for this function as well.

steps.title: Compare the statements
steps.content: Neither function has a minimum value at $-4$, or at any other value. The answer is D.
